children.text: A VFR flight when flying inside an ATS airspace classified as B has to maintain the following minima of flight visibility and distance from clouds:

answers: 5 km visibility, 1500 m horizontal and 300 m vertical from clouds
5 km below 3050 m (10.000 ft) AMSL 1500 m horizontal and 300 m vertical from clouds
8 km below 3050 m (10.000 ft) AMSL, 1 500 m horizontal and 300 m vertical from clouds
5 km below 3050 m (10.000 ft) AMSL and clear of clouds
correctAnswer: 5 km below 3050 m (10.000 ft) AMSL 1500 m horizontal and 300 m vertical from clouds

children.text: A VFR flight when flying inside an ATS airspace classified as C has to maintain the following minima of flight visibility and distance from clouds:

answers: 8 km at or above 3050 m (10.000 ft) AMSL 1500 m horizontal and 300 m vertical from clouds
5km at or above 3050 m (10.000 ft) AMSL 1500 m horizontal and 300 m vertical from clouds
5 NM at or above 3050 m (10.000 ft) AMSL, 1500 m horizontal and 300 m vertical from clouds
8 km at or above 3050 m (10.000 ft) AMSL, and clear of clouds
correctAnswer: 8 km at or above 3050 m (10.000 ft) AMSL 1500 m horizontal and 300 m vertical from clouds

children.text: An ATS airspace where IFR and VFR flights are permitted, all flights are subject to air traffic control service and IFR flights are separated from other IFR flights and from VFR flights VFR flights are separated from IFR flights and receive traffic information in respect of other VFR flights, is classified as:
An ATS airspace where IFR and VFR flights are permitted, all flights are subject to air traffic control service and are separated from each other is classified as

An ATS airspace where IFR and VFR flights are permitted and all flights are subject to air traffic control service. IFR flights are separated from other IFR flights and receive traffic information in respect of VFR flights. VFR flights receive traffic information in respect of all other flights, is classified as:

An ATS airspace where IFR and VFR are permitted IFR flights are subject to Air Trafic Control Service and are separated from other IFR flights. All flights receive traffic information as far as is practical, is classified as

An ATS airspace where IFR and VFR flights are permitted, all participating IFR flights receive an air traffic advisory service and all flights receive flight information service if requested, is classified
An ATS airspace where IFR and VFR are permitted and receive flight information service if  requested, is classified as
An air traffic control unit:

answers: must not ask an aircraft to change its call sign
may ask an aircraft to temporarily change its call sign for safety reasons when there is a risk of confusion between two or more similar all signs
may not ask an aircraft to change its call sign after accepting the flight plan
may require to change the call sign for safety reasons when there is a risk of confusion between two or more similar call signs providing the aircraft is on a repetitive flight plan
correctAnswer: may ask an aircraft to temporarily change its call sign for safety reasons when there is a risk of confusion between two or more similar all signs

children.text: The transfer of an aircraft from one ATC unit to another is done:

answers: by agreement with the receiving unit
automatically at the control zone boundary
correctAnswer: by agreement with the receiving unit

children.text: The longitudinal separation minima based on time between aircraft at same cruising level where navigation aids permit frequent determi-nation of position and speed, is:

The longitudinal separation minima based on time between aircraft at same cruising level where navigation aids permit frequent determi-nation of position and speed and the preceeding aircraft is maintaining a true airspeed of 20kt or more faster than the succeeding aircraft is:

The longitudinal separation minima based on time between aircraft at same cruising level where navigation aids permit frequent determi-nation of position and speed and the preceeding aircraft is maintaining a true airspeed of 40kt or more faster than the succeeding aircraft is:

The longitudinal separation minima between aircraft departed from the same aerodrome and following the same track, and the preceeding aircraft is maintaining a true airspeed of 20 kt or more faster than the succeeding aircraft, is:

The longitudinal separation minima between aircraft departed from the same aerodrome and following the same track, and the preceeding aircraft is maintaining a true airspeed of 40 kt or more faster than the succeeding aircraft, is:
The longitudinal separation minima based on distance using DME, and each aircraft "on track" uses DME stations, is:

answers: 20 NM when the leading aircraft maintains a true airspeed of 20 kt or more faster than the succeding aircraft

children.text: Area Control Centres issue clearances for the purpose of:
Clearances will be issued by an ATC unit for the purpose of:
You receive an IFR enroute clearance stating: "Clearance expires at 0920". What does it mean?

answers: The take off clearance is expected at 0920
If not airborne until 0920, a new clearance has to be issued
After 0920 return to the ramp and file a new flight plan
correctAnswer: If not airborne until 0920, a new clearance has to be issued

children.text: When are ATIS broadcasts updated?

answers: Upon receipt of any official weather, regardless of content change or reported values
Every 30 minutes if weather conditions are below those for VFR; otherwise hourly
Only when weather conditions change enough to require a change in the active runway or instrument approach in use
Only when the ceiling and/or visibility changes by a reportable value
correctAnswer: Upon receipt of any official weather, regardless of content change or reported values

children.text: Flight information service provided to flights shall include the provision of information concerning collision hazards to aircraft operating in airspace classes:
ATIS broadcast messages containing departure and arrival information should include cloud cover, when the clouds are:

answers: below 1 500 m (5.000 ft) or below the highest minimum sector altitude, whichever is the greater
below 2 000 m (6.000 ft) or below the highest minimum sector altitude, whichever is the greater
below 900 m (3.000 ft) or below the highest minimum sector altitude, whichever is the greater
correctAnswer: below 1 500 m (5.000 ft) or below the highest minimum sector altitude, whichever is the greater

children.text: When it becomes apparent that an aircraft is in difficulty, the decision to initiate the alert phases is the responsibility of the:
The Alerting Service is provided by:

answers: The ATC unit responsible for the aircraft at that moment, when it is provided with 121.5 MHz
The ATS unit responsible for the aircraft at that moment
correctAnswer: The ATS unit responsible for the aircraft at that moment

children.text: The phases related to an aircraft in emergency or believed in emergency are:

answers: uncertainty phase, urgency phase, distress phase
uncertainty phase, distress phase, urgency phase
uncertainty phase, alert phase, distress phase and urgency phase
uncertainty phase, alert phase, distress phase
correctAnswer: uncertainty phase, alert phase, distress phase

children.text: A radio communications, "Distress" differs from "Urgency" because in the first case:

answers: There is a serious and imminent danger requiring immediate assistance
The aeroplane or a passenger's safety require the flight immediately interrupted
The aeroplane has suffered damages which impair its fitness to fly
The aeroplane will not be able to reach a suitable aerodrome
correctAnswer: There is a serious and imminent danger requiring immediate assistance

children.text: Alert phase is defined as follows:

answers: An emergency event in which an aircraft and its occupants are considered to be threatened by a danger
A situation related to an aircraft which reports that the fuel on board is exhausted
A situation where an apprehension exists as to the safety of an aircraft and its occupants
A situation related to an aircraft and its occupants are considered to be in a state of emergency
correctAnswer: A situation where an apprehension exists as to the safety of an aircraft and its occupants

children.text: Which of the following statements regarding Alerting service is correct?

answers: Aircraft in the vicinity of an aircraft known or believed to be the subject of unlawful interference, shall be informed about this
The Alert phase is established when no communication has been received from an aircraft within a period of thirty minutes after the time a communication should have been received
Alerting Service and Flight Information Service are often provided by the same ATS unit
The distress phase is established when an aircraft is known or believed th be the subject of unlawful interference
correctAnswer: Alerting Service and Flight Information Service are often provided by the same ATS unit

children.text: When an aircraft is experiencing difficulties, triggering of the alert phase is the responsibility of:

answers: air traffic control and flight information centers
correctAnswer: air traffic control and flight information centers

children.text: The rule governing flight over water for a single engined aeroplane engaged in the public transport of passengers:

answers: limits flight to up to 8 NM from the nearest shore
does not permit such flight in any circumstances
limits such flight to a height sufficient to land safely if the engine fails
limits flight to up to 10 minutes flying time from the nearest shore
correctAnswer: limits such flight to a height sufficient to land safely if the engine fails

children.text: The period of validity for take-off slots assigned by CEU (flow control centre):

answers: depends on the type of flight (10 minutes for international flights, 5 minutes for domestic flights)

children.text: A flight plan shall be submitted prior to departure for a controlled flight at least:

answers: 30 minutes prior to leave the blocks
50 minutes prior to leave the blocks

children.text: If radio communication failure is experienced on an IFR flight in IMC, generally the pilot shall:

answers: Try to get contact on other frequencies either ground or aircraft stations
Land on the nearest suitable aerodrome and report the termination of the flight to ATc
Try to get contact on other frequencies either ground or aircraft stations - Transmit being indicating important details required 2 times
Transmit blind indicating details required at least 2 times
correctAnswer: Try to get contact on other frequencies either ground or aircraft stations - Transmit being indicating important details required 2 times

children.text: A strayed aircraft is:

answers: An aircraft which has deviated significantly from its intended track or which reports that it is lost
only that aircraft which reports that it is lost
only that aircraft which has deviated significantly its intended track
an aircraft in a given area but whose identity has not been established
correctAnswer: An aircraft which has deviated significantly from its intended track or which reports that it is lost

children.text: The pilot in command of an aircraft: 1 - must comply immediately to all instructions received from ATc 2 - is responsible only if he is the "pilot flying". 3 - may deviate from air regulations for safety reasons. 4 - may be exempt from air regulations in order to comply to an ATC instruction. 5 - may ask for the modification of an unsatisfactory clearance. Which of the following combinations contains all of the correct statements?

"ESSENTIAL TRAFFIC" is that controlled flight to which the provision of separation by ATC is applicable, but which, in relation to a particular controlled flight is not separated therefore by the appropriate separation minima. Whenever separation minima is not applied. The following flights are considered essential traffic one to each other.

answers: Controlled VFR flights and VFR flights
All IFR flight in controlled airspaces and controlled VFR
correctAnswer: All IFR flight in controlled airspaces and controlled VFR

children.text: When, in air space where VFR are permitted, the pilot in command of an IFR flight wishes to continue his flight in accordance with visual flight rules, until the destination is reached: 1 He must inform the control unit ("cancel IFR") 2 He must request and obtain clearance. 3 He may request his IFR flight plan to be changed to a VFR flight plan. 4 The flight plan automatically becomes a VFR flight plan. The correct combination of statements is:
In the event of a delay of a controlled flight, the submitted flight plan should be amended or cancelled and a new flight plan submitted when the delay is:

answers: 30 minutes in excess of the estimated time off blocks
60 minutes in excess of the estimated time off blocks
30 minutes in excess of the estimated time of departure
60 minutes in excess of the estimated time of departure
correctAnswer: 30 minutes in excess of the estimated time off blocks

children.text: Which is the content of section 2 of Air-Report (AIREP)?

answers: Present position, estimated time of arrival (ETA)
Estimated time over FIR boundary, endurance

children.text: The position reports shall contain the following elements of information in the order listed:

answers: Aircraft identification, position, time, flight level or altitude, next position and time over and ensuing significant point
Aircraft identification, position, time, flight level or altitude, next position and time over
Aircraft identification, position, time, true air speed, flight level or altitude, next position and time over
Aircraft identification, position, flight level or altitude,time, next position and time over and ensuing significant point
correctAnswer: Aircraft identification, position, time, flight level or altitude, next position and time over and ensuing significant point

children.text: Who is responsible for an ATC clearance to be safe in respect to terrain clearance?

answers: The air traffic service reporting office when accepting the flight plan

children.text: Which letter is used in a flight plan to indicate that the flight commences in accordance with VFR and subsequently changes to IFR?
Which letter is used in a flight plan to indicate that the flight commences in accordance with IFR and subsequently changes to VFR?
In the event of a delay for an uncontrolled flight which a flight plan has been submitted, the flight plan should be amended or a new flight plan submitted and the old one cancelled, when:

answers: The delay is more than 30 minutes of the estimated time off departure
The delay is more than 30 minutes of the estimated time off-blocks
The delay is more than 60 minutes of the estimated time off-blocks
The delay is more than 60 minutes of the estimated time of departure
correctAnswer: The delay is more than 60 minutes of the estimated time off-blocks

children.text: A pilot receiving an IFR clearance from ATC should:

answers: Read back those parts containing level assignments, vectors or any part requiring verification
Read back the entire clearance as required by regulation
Read back the initial route clearance, level assignments and transponder codes
correctAnswer: Read back the entire clearance as required by regulation

children.text: A Special Air Report comprises a number of sections. In section I the pilot fills in:

answers: a position report, including aircraft identification, height, position and time
correctAnswer: a position report, including aircraft identification, height, position and time

children.text: The planned cruising speed for the first leg or all of the cruising portion of the flight must be entered in the speed box of a flight plan form. This speed is the:
The "estimated total time" in block 16 of a VFR flight plan is the estimated time:

answers: required by the aircraft from the moment it moves by its own power until it stops at the end of the flight (block time)
of endurance at cruising power taking into account pressure and temperature on that day
required by the aircraft from brake release at take-off until landing
required by the aircraft from take-off to arrive overhead the destination airport
correctAnswer: required by the aircraft from take-off to arrive overhead the destination airport

children.text: The letter "L" is written in the wake turbulence box of a flight plan form when the maximum certified take-off weight of an aircraft is less than or equal to:

answers: 5 700 kg for airplanes and 2 700 kg for helicopters

children.text: If no ICAO identifier has been attributed to an alternate airport (box 16) of a flight plan form...

answers: write XXXX in box 16 and indicate in box 18 (additional information) DEGT/followed by the name of the airport
write ZZZZ in box 16 and indicate in box 18 (additional information) DEGT/followed by the name of the airport
write ZZZZ in box 16 and indicate in box 18 (additional information) ALTN/followed by the name of the airport
write XXXX in box 16 and indicate in box 18 (additional information) ALTN/followed by the name of the airport
correctAnswer: write ZZZZ in box 16 and indicate in box 18 (additional information) ALTN/followed by the name of the airport

children.text: The longitudinal separation minima based on DME, and each aircraft "on track" uses DME stations, is:

answers: 10 NM provided that the leading aircraft maintains a true airspeed of 10 kt or more faster than the succeding aircraft
10 NM provided that the leading aircraft maintains a true airspeed of 40 kt or more faster than the succeding aircraft
20 NM provided that the leading aircraft maintains a true airspeed of 10 kt or more faster than the succeding aircraft
10 NM provided that the leading aircraft maintains a true airspeed of 20 kt or more faster than the succeding aircraft
correctAnswer: 10 NM provided that the leading aircraft maintains a true airspeed of 20 kt or more faster than the succeding aircraft

children.text: Which procedure you follow if during an IFR flight in VMC you have two way communication failure?

answers: Continue the flight maintaining VMC and land as soon as practicable
Maintain your assigned level and route and land at the nearest aerodrome that has VMC conditions
Return to the aerodrome of departure
Continue the flight at the assigned level and route; start approach at your ETa
correctAnswer: Continue the flight maintaining VMC and land as soon as practicable

children.text: Track separation between aircraft using the same NDB shall be applied requiring the aircraft to fly:

answers: At least 45° separated at a distance of 15 NM or more from the facility
At least 30° separated at a distance of 15 miles or more from the facility
At least 30° separated at a distance of 15 NM or more from the facility
At least 15° separated at a distance of 15 NM or more from the facility
correctAnswer: At least 30° separated at a distance of 15 NM or more from the facility

children.text: Track separation between aircraft using the same FIX shall be applied requiring the aircraft to fly:

answers: At least 30° separated at a distance of 15 miles or more from the FIX
At least 30° separated at a distance of 15 NM or more from the FIX
At least 45° separated at a distance of 15 miles or more from the FIX
At least 45° separated at a distance of 15 NM or more from the fix
correctAnswer: At least 45° separated at a distance of 15 NM or more from the fix

children.text: If an ATC clearance is not suitable to the pilot in command of an aircraft:

answers: The pilot should propose another clearance to the ATC concerned
He may request and, if practicable, obtain an amended clearance
He may request another clearance and the ATC concerned has to accept the pilot request
The pilot has to accept the ATC clearance because it has been based on the flight plan filed with ATc
correctAnswer: He may request and, if practicable, obtain an amended clearance

children.text: The "VMC and own separation" ATC clearance is used for a controlled flight to cross the level of another controlled flight when:

answers: Requested by the pilot and during the day light
Requested by the pilot, during the day light and authorized by the state overflown
Requested by the pilot and authorized by the state overflown
correctAnswer: Requested by the pilot, during the day light and authorized by the state overflown

children.text: Normally all turns, which are requested by a radar controller have to be executed as:

answers: Standard rate turns if not otherwise instructed by ATc
correctAnswer: Standard rate turns if not otherwise instructed by ATc

children.text: What are the controlled IFR separation methods applied by ATC?

explanation: QDB Information: Excerpt from Dictionary of Aeronautical Terms, published by aviation supplies and academics, Inc; Newcastle, Washington, ISBN 1-56027-287-2 "A method of separating aircraft in a composite route system where, by management of route and altitude assignments, a combination of half the lateral minimum specified for the area concerned and half the vertical minimum is applied".

children.text: The vertical IFR separation minimum being applied by ATC within a controlled airspace below FL 290 is:
The vertical IFR separation minimum being applied by ATC within a controlled airspace above FL 290 is:
Track separation between aircraft using the same VOR shall be applied requiring the aircraft to fly:

answers: At least 45° separated at a distance of 15 NM or more from the facility
At least 15° separated at a distance of 15 NM or more from the facility
At least 30° separated at a distance of 15 NM or more from the facility
At least 15° separated at a distance of 15 miles or more from the facility
correctAnswer: At least 15° separated at a distance of 15 NM or more from the facility

children.text: Flying exactly on your current flight plan route, you receive and acknowledge the following  instruction from the radar controller: "Turn immediately, continue heading 050° until further advised". Time now is 18:36 UTC. At 18:37 UTC you find out that radio communication cannot be established again and you have to return to your current flight plan route:

answers: With an intercept of at least 45°
With an intercept of at least 30°
With an intercept of 20° or more

children.text: Above flight level FL 290 the vertical flight separation between aircraft on the same direction is:
A "RNAV" distance based separation minimum may be used at the time the level is crossed, provided that each aircraft reports its distance to or from the same "on track" way-point. This minimum is:
A VFR flight constitutes essential traffic to other VFR flights, when operating in controlled airspaced classified as:
One minute separation may be used between departing aircraft if they are to fly on tracks diverging by at least:
Two minutes separation may be used between departing aircraft if they are to fly on the same track, when:

answers: The preceeding aircraft is 30 kt or more faster than the following aircraft
The preceeding aircraft is 40 kt or more faster than the following aircraft
The preceeding aircraft is 10 kt or more faster than the following aircraft
The preceeding aircraft is 20 kt or more faster than the following aircraft
correctAnswer: The preceeding aircraft is 40 kt or more faster than the following aircraft

children.text: The separation method whereby the vertical and horizontal separation may be reduced till a maximum of half the standard criteria is called

Whenever unlawful interference with an aircraft is suspected, and where automatic distinct display of SSR Mode A code 7500 and code 7700 is not provided, the radar controller shall attempt to verify this suspicion by:

answers: Setting the SSR decoder to mode A 7500 then to standby and thereafter to code 7700
Setting the SSR decoder to mode A code 7000 and thereafter to code 7500
Setting the SSR decoder to mode A 7700 then to standby and thereafter to code 7500
Setting the SSR decoder to mode A code 7500 and thereafter to code 7700
correctAnswer: Setting the SSR decoder to mode A code 7500 and thereafter to code 7700

children.text: When the Mach number technique (MNT) is being applied, and the preceding aircraft shall maintain a mach number equal to or greater than the following aircraft a RNAV distance based separation minimum may be used on the same direction tracks in lieu of 10 minutes longitudinal separation minimum. The distance is:

Longitudinal separation minima based on distance using DME for aircraft at the same cruising level and track, provided that each aircraft utilizes "on Track" DME stations and separation is checked by obtaining simultaneous DME readings, is:

Longitudinal separation minima based on time for aircraft at the same cruising level when navigation aids permit frequent determination of position and speed provided that the preceding aircraft is maintaining a true air speed of 40 Kt or more faster than the succeeding aircraft will be

Longitudinal separation minima based on time for aircraft at the same cruising level when navigation aids permit frequent determination of position and speed provided that the preceding aircraft is maintaining a true air speed of 20 Kt or more faster than the succeeding aircraft will be
Longitudinal separation minima based on time for aircraft at the same cruising level when navigation aids permit frequent determination of position and speed will be:
Repetitive flight plans (RPL's) shall not be used for flights operated regularly on the same day(s) of consecutive weeks and:

answers: On at least ten occasions or every day over a period of at least ten consecutive days
On at least ten occasions or every day over a period of at least 20 consecutive days
correctAnswer: On at least ten occasions or every day over a period of at least ten consecutive days

children.text: The minimum sector altitude provides 300 metres obstacle clearance within how many miles radius from the navigation facility upon which the instrument approach procedure is predicated:
"Time Approach Procedure" is used as necessary to expedite the approach of a number of arriving aircraft. This will be obtained requesting aircraft:

answers: To apply a step down descent between aircraft in the approach sequence
To maintain a specified speed during the approach procedure
To pass the specified point inbound at the previously notified time
correctAnswer: To pass the specified point inbound at the previously notified time

children.text: During a take-off into IMC conditions with low ceiling the pilot should contact departure control:

answers: When clear of the airport and established on the first heading given in the clearance

children.text: A so called "Visual Approach" can be performed:

answers: during IFR flights, if the cloudbase is 1000 ft more than the appropriate DA or MDA for that procedure
during IFR flights, if there is permanent sight on the movement area and the underlying ground
as in above, but in addition there should be a visibility of 5,5 km or more
during IFR and VFR flights in VMC
correctAnswer: during IFR flights, if there is permanent sight on the movement area and the underlying ground

children.text: An approaching aircraft may descent below the MSA if:

answers: the pilot has the field and the underlying terrain in sight and will keep it in sight
the pilot is following the published approach procedure

children.text: The EAT has to be transmitted to the pilot as soon as possible, in case the expected delay is:
Which statement is correct? During a "Visual Approach" in Controlled Airspace (Class C):

answers: ATC will apply separation with other arriving traffic
ATC will apply separation with other traffic
ATC will apply separation only with other IFR-traffic
the pilot to apply separation with other traffic
correctAnswer: ATC will apply separation with other traffic

children.text: If an arriving aircraft is making a straight in approach a departing aircraft may take off in any direction

answers: until three minutes before the arriving aircraft is estimated to be over the instrument runway
until two minutes before the arriving aircraft is estimated to be over the instrument runway
until ten minutes before the arriving aircraft is estimated to be over the instrument runway
until five minutes before the arriving aircraft is estimated to be over the instrument runway
correctAnswer: until five minutes before the arriving aircraft is estimated to be over the instrument runway

children.text: At the commencement of final approach, if the controller possesses wind information in the form of components, significant changes in the mean surface wind direction and speed shall be transmitted to aircraft. The mean cross-wind component significant change is:

At the commencement of final approach, if the controller possesses wind information in the form of components, significant changes in the mean surface wind direction and speed shall be transmitted to aircraft. The mean tail-wind component significant change is:

At the commencement of final approach, if the controller possesses wind information in the form of components, significant changes in the mean surface wind direction and speed shall be transmitted to aircraft. The mean head-wind component significant change is:

A minimum vertical separation shall be provided until aircraft are etablished inbound on the ILS localizer course and/or MLS final approach track. This minimum is, when independent parallel approaches are being conducted:

A minimum radar separation shall be provided until aircraft are etablished inbound on the ILS localizer course and/or MLS final approach track. This minimum is, when independent parallel approaches are being conducted:
Independent parallel approaches may be conducted to parallel runways provided that:

answers: the missed approach track for one approach diverges by at least 30° (degrees) from the missed approach track of the adjacent approach
the missed approach track for one approach diverges by at least 20° (degrees) from the missed approach track of the adjacent approach
the missed approach track for one approach diverges by at least 45° (degrees) from the missed approach track of the adjacent approach
the missed approach track for one approach diverges by at least 25° (degrees) from the missed approach track of the adjacent approach
correctAnswer: the missed approach track for one approach diverges by at least 30° (degrees) from the missed approach track of the adjacent approach

children.text: When independent parallel approaches are being conducted and vectoring to intercept the ILS localizer course or MLS final approach track,the final vector shall be such as to enable the aircraft to intercept the ILS localizer course or MLS final approach track at an angle not greater than:
Independent parallel approaches may be conducted to parallel runways provided that a no transgression zone (NTZ) of at least:

answers: 710 m is established between extended runway centre lines and as is depicted on the radar display
610 m is established between extended runway centre lines and as is depicted on the radar display
600 m is established between extended runway centre lines and as is depicted on the radar display
500 m is established between extended runway centre lines and as is depicted on the radar display
correctAnswer: 610 m is established between extended runway centre lines and as is depicted on the radar display

children.text: When independent parallel approaches are being conducted to parallel runways and vectoring to intercept the ILS localizer course or MLS final approach track, the vector shall be such as to enable the aircraft to be established on the ILS localizer course or MLS final approach track in  level flight for:

answers: at least 1.5 NM prior to intercepting the ILS glide path or specified MLS elevation angle
at least 2.0 NM prior to intercepting the ILS glide path or specified MLS elevation angle
at least 2.5 NM prior to intercepting the ILS glide path or specified MLS elevation angle
at least 3.0 NM prior to intercepting the ILS glide path or specified MLS elevation angle
correctAnswer: at least 2.0 NM prior to intercepting the ILS glide path or specified MLS elevation angle

children.text: Dependent parallel approaches may be conducted to parallel runways provided that: the missed approach track for one approach diverges by:

answers: at least 45° (degrees) from the missed approach track of the adjacent approach
at least 30° (degrees) from the missed approach track of the adjacent approach
at least 25° (degrees) from the missed approach track of the adjacent approach
at least 15° (degrees) from the missed approach track of the adjacent approach
correctAnswer: at least 30° (degrees) from the missed approach track of the adjacent approach

children.text: For an IFR flight to an airport equipped with navaids, the estimated time of arrival is the estimated time at which the aircraft:

answers: will stop on the parking area
will leave the initial approach fix to start the final approach
will arrive overhead the initial approach fix
correctAnswer: will arrive overhead the initial approach fix

children.text: During an arrival procedure under an IFR flight plan in VMC conditions, traffic avoidance is the responsibility of:
When a runway is 2 000 metres in length, and taxi holding positions have not been established, aircraft shall not be held closer to the runway in use more than:
Which statement regarding approach control service is correct?

answers: During a visual approach an aircraft is maintaining its own separation
An approach sequence shall be established according to the sequence of initial radio contact between aircraft and approach control
Approach control have to advise the aircraft operators about substantial delays in departure in any event when they are expected to exceed 45 minutes
If it is anticipated that an aircraft has to hold for 30 minutes or more, an Expected Approach Time will be transmitted by the most expeditious means to the aircraft
correctAnswer: If it is anticipated that an aircraft has to hold for 30 minutes or more, an Expected Approach Time will be transmitted by the most expeditious means to the aircraft

children.text: Which of the following statements regarding aerodrome control service is correct?

answers: Suspension of VFR operations can not be initiated by the aerodrome controller
An aircraft entering the traffic circuit without permission of ATC, will be cleared to land if this is desirable
The aerodrome control service is a service provided for the purpose of preventing collisions between aircraft on the movement area
ATC permission is required for entering the apron with a vehicule
correctAnswer: An aircraft entering the traffic circuit without permission of ATC, will be cleared to land if this is desirable

children.text: Lights on and in the vicinity of aerodromes may be turned off, provided that they can be again brought into operation:

answers: At least one hour before the expected arrival of an aircraft
At least 30 minutes before the expected arrival of an aircraft
At least 15 minutes before the expected arrival of an aircraft
At least 5 minutes before the expected arrival of an aircraft
correctAnswer: At least one hour before the expected arrival of an aircraft

children.text: What is the minimum wake turbulence separation criteria when a light aircraft is taking off behind a medium aircraft and both are using the same runway?
